title: Create build targets
description: Create the Makefile targets for building server and client from the planned code layout. Keep compiler flags suitable for a Linux C++ course project.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN make runs THEN server and client binaries are built from code/src and code/include; WHEN make clean runs THEN generated binaries and object files are removed; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:268
review_initial_requirements: Review file paths against the approved code directory before adding extra directories.
review_regression_requirements: Verify build output names and run commands match ./server 8888 and ./client 127.0.0.1 8888 alice.

title: Write project README
description: Write code/README.md with compile instructions, server and client startup commands, client command syntax, expected behavior, and manual testing steps.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN README is opened THEN a user can compile, run server, run at least two clients, send group chat, send private chat, list users, quit, and inspect server.log; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:287
review_initial_requirements: Review README instructions against actual command names before marking complete.
review_regression_requirements: Verify README does not document unimplemented features such as account passwords, database storage, chat history query, GUI, or admin kick.
notes: module:delivery; source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md; picked_reason:README can document approved commands and manual workflow before Makefile integration; done_at:2026-06-22; skills_used:documentation-and-adrs;verification-before-completion; assumption:DELIV-01 will provide approved make/server/client commands documented here; evidence:test -s code/README.md and rg required build/run/command/manual/log/non-goal text exited 0; mcp_used:; continue_now:DELIV-01 Create build targets pending server/client integration

title: Prepare course paper outline
description: Create a course-paper outline document that matches the approved project and lists the required screenshots and code sections to capture after implementation. This is an outline and evidence checklist, not the final paper.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the outline exists THEN it contains background, architecture, TCP socket principle, select design, protocol design, server implementation, client implementation, key code screenshot list, test screenshot list, and summary sections; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:297
review_initial_requirements: Review the outline against the course submission requirements before adding prose.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the outline calls out code screenshots, runtime screenshots, logs, and test evidence from the approved design.
notes: module:delivery; source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md; picked_reason:paper outline is independent of pending server/client implementation and can be closed while parallel workers run; done_at:2026-06-22; skills_used:documentation-and-adrs;verification-before-completion; evidence:test -s code/course_paper_outline.md and rg required section headings exited 0; mcp_used:; continue_now:DELIV-04 Create validation checklist

title: Create validation checklist
description: Create a validation checklist for manual end-to-end testing of the chatroom. The checklist must mirror the approved functional, abnormal, and result-check tests.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN checklist exists THEN it covers single-client login, multi-client group chat, private chat, LIST, duplicate nickname, not-logged-in command, invalid command, abnormal close, long message, terminal output, and server.log; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:243
review_initial_requirements: Review checklist items against approved test section and acceptance standards.
review_regression_requirements: Verify every approved test condition has a pass/fail evidence slot and no fake automated pass is claimed before execution.
notes: module:delivery; source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md; picked_reason:manual validation checklist is independent and can be closed while implementation workers run; done_at:2026-06-22; skills_used:verification-before-completion; evidence:test -s code/validation_checklist.md and rg required scenarios/output/log sections exited 0; mcp_used:; continue_now:DELIV-01 Create build targets pending server/client integration

title: Review delivery artifacts against course requirements
description: Use a same-model sub-agent review to compare build, README, paper outline, and validation checklist artifacts against the approved course-delivery claims.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all non-review issues before this row are closed THEN run same-model sub-agent review against source-specific claim/evidence checks; WHEN gaps or overstated claims are found THEN append follow-up issues and REVIEW-02; WHEN no gaps remain THEN close the CSV.
review_initial_requirements: Verify all prior non-review rows are closed before running this review.
review_regression_requirements: Check that Makefile builds server and client with approved run commands; Check that README enables a new user to compile, run, and test the chatroom; Check that the paper outline covers key code screenshots and runtime/test screenshots; Check that validation checklist mirrors approved functional, abnormal, and log checks without claiming unrun tests.
